title: Quarantine Bureau state-root legacy artifacts
goal: Restore a healthy Bureau state root by classifying the unknown entry and moving confirmed legacy/archive candidates into a digest-manifested reversible quarantine outside the active root, without deletion.

acceptance.assertion: The 38 doctor-classified archive candidates and the unknown weltgewebe-audit entry are re-read live; every moved entry has a documented class, original path, type, size and SHA-256 or deterministic directory manifest.
Confirmed legacy entries are moved atomically into a timestamped quarantine outside the active Bureau state root; no entry is deleted and a collision-safe rollback map is written.

Bureau service/process use is checked before mutation, SQLite database/sidecars and known envelope/receipt/review directories remain untouched, and post-move database integrity plus Bureau doctor/check are green.
The weltgewebe-audit entry is retained if ownership or replay value cannot be proven; uncertainty must not be converted into deletion or silent archival.
Any later permanent deletion requires a separate retention decision and explicit authorization after a quarantine observation period.

metadata.reason_not_executed_now: State relocation is a separate host mutation and must not be mixed into the registry reconcile or the active Heimlern consumer ball.
metadata.outcome: Moved 38 doctor-classified legacy artifacts plus one classified static Weltgewebe audit bundle by same-filesystem atomic rename into a timestamped quarantine outside the active Bureau state root. No deletion occurred. The active root now contains exactly the six canonical entries and Bureau doctor/database checks are healthy.

verified_acceptance.classify-before-move: The live doctor list yielded exactly 38 known archive candidates. The unknown directory was inspected as six schema-v2 static audit event JSON files, with no repository/state-root references, suspicious credential keys or open file descriptors, and classified as a replay-preserved legacy audit bundle.
verified_acceptance.reversible-quarantine: All 39 entries were moved by same-filesystem os.rename into one timestamped 0700 quarantine. A 0600 rollback map records every source, destination and file/tree digest. No deletion occurred.
verified_acceptance.runtime-safety: No process held a planned source entry open. bureau.sqlite3, WAL/SHM, envelopes, receipts and reviews were excluded. Post-move SQLite integrity/quick_check are ok and Bureau doctor reports state_root_hygiene healthy.
verified_acceptance.unknown-fail-closed: The previously unknown directory was not deleted; its six event files and replay value remain byte-preserved in quarantine with file hashes.
verified_acceptance.deletion-separate: The quarantine result states deletion=false. Permanent deletion remains unauthorized and requires a separate retention decision.

metadata.self_review: PASS: exact candidate set, protected entries, filesystem identity, process file descriptors, pre/post hashes, rollback map, database integrity and doctor state were checked. No content or entry was deleted.
